confidence_scale.DIRECT: Explicitly documented in retrieved corpus as decorator or mechanism.
confidence_scale.INFERRED: Strongly implied by mechanism, sequencing, or architectural mapping.
confidence_scale.SPECULATIVE: Architecturally useful wrapper or extension lacking direct first-class documentation.

design_rules: Prefer orthogonal classes over proliferating same-class variants.
Mark decorators as DIRECT, INFERRED, or SPECULATIVE.
Do not treat every workflow pattern as a cognitive decorator.
Evaluate classes with null baselines and catastrophic failure triggers.
Keep privilege and security decorators separate from expressive decorators.

classes.purpose: Resolve instruction conflicts across sources by explicit authority or trust level.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Instruction-hierarchy collapse, position bias, unsafe obedience to low-trust text.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Overly rigid authority structures that ignore useful local overrides.
classes.orthogonality_note: Authority-plane controls determine which instruction source wins; they are not reasoning-depth or style controls.
classes.null_baseline: No explicit privilege declarations; compare susceptibility to authoritative-sounding retrieved text.

decorators.name: +++Privilege
decorators.mechanism: Assigns explicit authority levels to instruction sources so higher-ranked sources prevail under conflict.
failure_modes: stale high-tier instructions dominating fresh evidence

decorators.name: +++TrustTier
decorators.mechanism: Assigns trust weight to retrieved text, tool output, or sub-agent output to prevent self-promotion by low-trust context.

decorators.name: +++InstructionRank
decorators.mechanism: Declares the conflict-resolution policy used when ranked sources disagree.

decorators.name: +++DynamicPrivilege
decorators.mechanism: Temporarily adjusts source authority under explicit conditions such as expert-reviewed retrieval or human escalation.

classes.purpose: Limit external influence on consequential actions and reduce prompt-injection attack surface.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Prompt injection, unsafe tool use, hidden context contamination.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Useful evidence loss, underpowered autonomy, safe-but-empty outputs.
classes.orthogonality_note: Action-plane controls gate tools, execution, and context transfer rather than deciding epistemic truth or output style.
classes.null_baseline: No action gating or context minimization; test injected instructions embedded in retrieved documents or structured fields.

decorators.name: +++ActionSelector
decorators.mechanism: Restricts tool use to an allowlisted set and denies unlisted actions.

decorators.name: +++ContextMinimize
decorators.mechanism: Passes only task-local context into high-consequence steps to reduce hidden contamination and attack surface.

decorators.name: +++PlanThenExecute
decorators.mechanism: Separates planning from execution so untrusted context can shape analysis without directly triggering action.

decorators.name: +++DualAgentBoundary
decorators.mechanism: Uses separate planner and executor agents so raw adversarial content never reaches the execution agent unchanged.

decorators.name: +++CodeThenExecute
decorators.mechanism: Constrains generated code to an isolated reviewable execution path.

decorators.name: +++InjectionAudit
decorators.mechanism: Records attempted override patterns and security-boundary crossings for post-hoc analysis.

classes.purpose: Control pipeline stages, handoffs, branching, and agent coordination.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Stage collapse, repeated work, poor evidence routing, handoff confusion.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Excess orchestration overhead, latency, brittle pipelines.
classes.orthogonality_note: These decorators define process topology and handoffs, not cognitive intensity.
classes.null_baseline: Monolithic prompt with no stage separation; compare evidence routing quality and repeated work.

decorators.name: +++StageRoute
decorators.mechanism: Declares explicit stage order and pass criteria between pipeline segments.

decorators.name: +++MapReduce
decorators.mechanism: Distributes extraction or analysis across shards, then merges claims with deduplication.

decorators.name: +++ShardRouter
decorators.mechanism: Partitions inputs by topic, source, or task type before routing them to specialized processing paths.

decorators.name: +++MycelialRoute
decorators.mechanism: Coordinates distributed agents through mesh-like exchange rather than strict central sequencing.

decorators.name: +++Rewrite
decorators.mechanism: Reformulates queries or stage inputs when failure conditions or contradictions are detected.

decorators.name: +++AgentHandoff
decorators.mechanism: Defines explicit handoff contracts between agents to preserve routing semantics and accountability.

classes.purpose: Modify how the agent asks, clarifies, rewrites, imports, or adapts to the user.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Ambiguity collapse, poor query reformulation, weak user-model alignment.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Excessive questioning, conversational drag, delayed task progress.
classes.orthogonality_note: Interaction controls shape the epistemic exchange with the user before and during generation, not merely the final writing style.
classes.null_baseline: Immediate answer with no clarification or decomposition; compare misread-task frequency.

decorators.name: +++ClarifyFirst
decorators.mechanism: Prompts bounded clarification before execution when ambiguity is high.

decorators.name: +++AskBack
decorators.mechanism: Requests missing constraints when they emerge during reasoning rather than only at task start.

decorators.name: +++Import
decorators.mechanism: Imports domain framing or conceptual scaffolds before detailed work begins.

decorators.name: +++Rewrite
decorators.mechanism: Rewrites the user task into better-structured sub-questions or task plans.

decorators.name: +++Tone
decorators.mechanism: Adapts interpersonal register to user needs during the exchange, distinct from report-level style enforcement.

decorators.name: +++AdaptiveRegister
decorators.mechanism: Continuously adapts questioning and framing based on inferred user expertise and edits.

classes.purpose: Manage persistence, memory scope, anchor refresh, and cross-turn carry-forward rules.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Context rot, provenance washout, memory contamination across turns.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Stale anchors, excessive reinjection, resistance to useful updating.
classes.orthogonality_note: Session controls manage persistence and forgetting across turns rather than within-turn reasoning form.
classes.null_baseline: No anchor refresh or carry-forward policy; compare long-horizon drift and stale-context contamination.

decorators.name: +++ContextLock
decorators.mechanism: Reinjects core invariants periodically to resist context drift and recency washout.

decorators.name: +++TemporalAnchor
decorators.mechanism: Tags and stabilizes time-sensitive provenance so it survives long-horizon context turnover.

decorators.name: +++CarryForward
decorators.mechanism: Carries only selected validated content into future turns according to explicit rules.

decorators.name: +++SessionScope
decorators.mechanism: Defines whether session memory is task-local, shared, or hard-isolated.

decorators.name: +++ResetBoundary
decorators.mechanism: Clears designated memory regions when a task, phase, or trust boundary completes.

decorators.name: +++MemoryPromotion
decorators.mechanism: Promotes validated content from working memory into longer-lived stores only after passing a gate.

classes.purpose: Control tone, style, compression, rhetorical density, and readability.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Low readability, inconsistent communication style, bloated outputs.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Style obedience without substance, loss of nuance.
classes.orthogonality_note: Expressive controls should shape final surface form and remain lower authority than security, privilege, and reasoning controls.
classes.null_baseline: No explicit style constraints; compare readability and semantic retention versus constrained finalization.

decorators.name: +++Tone
decorators.mechanism: Controls formal tone and communicative stance in final output.

decorators.name: +++Voice
decorators.mechanism: Controls narrative viewpoint and attribution stance.

decorators.name: +++AdjectivalBound
decorators.mechanism: Limits evaluative or decorative language to preserve semantic density.

decorators.name: +++Compress
decorators.mechanism: Reduces output length while preserving designated semantic features such as evidence traces.

decorators.name: +++Markdown
decorators.mechanism: Constrains output to a specific readable markup structure.

decorators.name: +++RhetoricalDensity
decorators.mechanism: Balances information density and hedging level to improve readability without diluting content.

classes.purpose: Handle rollback, error repair, scar maintenance, and post-failure continuation.
classes.main_failure_if_absent: Linear failure accumulation, dead-end states, brittle long-horizon execution.
classes.main_risk_if_overused: Oscillation, repetitive retries, overconservative fallback behavior.
classes.orthogonality_note: Recovery controls activate after failure detection and govern rollback or degradation paths rather than primary execution flow.
classes.null_baseline: No rollback or fallback controls; compare dead-end frequency and cascade failures in long-horizon tasks.

decorators.name: +++SagaRecovery
decorators.mechanism: Rolls back to the last sound state using compensating actions rather than failing terminally.

decorators.name: +++DebridementProtocol
decorators.mechanism: Prunes harmful failure residue or symbolic scar tissue that would distort future reasoning.

decorators.name: +++RetryPolicy
decorators.mechanism: Bounds retries and specifies escape behavior when repeated attempts fail.

decorators.name: +++FallbackMode
decorators.mechanism: Routes execution to a safe reduced-capability mode when cascade failure is detected.

decorators.name: +++ScarInoculation
decorators.mechanism: Injects synthetic failures during evaluation to build resilience against known failure families.

mutex.rationale: Debridement prunes symbolic scar tissue while EpistemicEscrow depends on its integrity as a divergence reference.
High mutation pressure and evaluative-language suppression can create contradictory logit pressure in dense entity contexts.

requires.rationale: Security enforcement is more coherent when authority ordering is already explicit.
Temporal provenance survives better when core anchors are periodically reinjected.
